title: Add NetworkPolicy for operator pod egress restriction
description:
# GitHub Issue #127: Add NetworkPolicy for operator pod egress restriction

**Category**: security | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: The NetworkPolicy sub-reconciler creates policies for Keystone API pods but not for the operator Deployment itself. Add a NetworkPolicy that restricts the operator pod's egress to only the Kubernetes API server and the namespaces it needs to communicate with.

**Motivation**: The operator pod currently has unrestricted network egress. If compromised, it could be used to reach arbitrary destinations. Restricting egress to only necessary endpoints reduces the blast radius.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/chart/templates/`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy.go`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] NetworkPolicy restricts operator pod egress to kube-apiserver CIDR
- [ ] DNS egress to kube-dns is permitted
- [ ] Operator functions correctly with the restricted policy
- [ ] E2E tests pass with the NetworkPolicy applied

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/127

stories.title: Cluster operator hardens keystone-operator egress via Helm
stories.want: a NetworkPolicy shipped by the keystone-operator Helm chart that restricts the operator pod's egress to only the Kubernetes API server and DNS (CC-0090)
stories.so_that: a compromised operator pod cannot reach arbitrary internal or external destinations, containing blast radius to resources the operator was already authorised to mutate via the API server
criteria: helm template with networkPolicy.enabled=true produces exactly one NetworkPolicy of apiVersion networking.k8s.io/v1 targeting the operator pod via selectorLabels (CC-0090, REQ-001)
helm template with networkPolicy.enabled=false (default) produces zero NetworkPolicy objects — opt-in, never opt-out (CC-0090, REQ-001)
The rendered NetworkPolicy declares PolicyTypes [Ingress, Egress] so default-deny applies to both directions (CC-0090, REQ-002)
Egress contains a rule for UDP+TCP port 53 to a DNS peer selector (kube-system namespaceSelector + k8s-app=kube-dns podSelector by default) (CC-0090, REQ-003)
Egress contains a rule for TCP 443/6443 to the kube-apiserver ipBlock(s) configured in values (CC-0090, REQ-004)

stories.title: Chart maintainer configures kube-apiserver endpoint for varied environments
stories.want: the NetworkPolicy to accept a list of kube-apiserver CIDRs and ports via values.yaml, with strict schema validation (CC-0090)
stories.so_that: the same chart works across kind/kubeadm (10.96.0.1:6443), managed offerings (10.0.0.1:443, private subnet CIDRs), and bare-metal clusters without forking
criteria: values.yaml exposes networkPolicy.kubeApiServer.cidrs as a list of CIDR strings and networkPolicy.kubeApiServer.ports as a list of integers (CC-0090, REQ-005)
values.schema.json validates each CIDR against a strict ipv4/ipv6 CIDR pattern and each port as integer in [1,65535] (CC-0090, REQ-005)
helm template fails when networkPolicy.enabled=true and networkPolicy.kubeApiServer.cidrs is empty — failing closed, never open (CC-0090, REQ-006)
helm template fails when networkPolicy.enabled=true and networkPolicy.kubeApiServer.ports is empty — failing closed (CC-0090, REQ-006)
values.yaml documents a working kind default (cidrs: [10.96.0.1/32], ports: [6443]) commented out, not applied by default (CC-0090, REQ-005)

stories.title: Cluster operator preserves webhook admission and metrics scraping
stories.want: the NetworkPolicy to permit inbound webhook calls from the API server and inbound metrics scrapes from Prometheus when enabled (CC-0090)
stories.so_that: turning on egress restriction does not silently break the validating/defaulting webhook (blocking all Keystone CR creates) or blind Prometheus to operator metrics
criteria: When webhook.enabled=true, the NetworkPolicy contains an ingress rule on TCP 9443 from the configured networkPolicy.webhookClients peer (default: empty ipBlock list + apiServer cidrs) (CC-0090, REQ-007)
When webhook.enabled=false, no ingress rule for port 9443 is rendered (CC-0090, REQ-007)
Ingress rule on TCP metrics.port is rendered only when networkPolicy.allowMetricsFrom is non-empty, selecting by namespaceSelector+podSelector (CC-0090, REQ-008)
Ingress rule on TCP 8081 (health) is not exposed externally — health is kubelet-local and permitted by default via hostNetwork exemption NOT needed; no ingress rule added (CC-0090, REQ-008)
When rbac.namespaceScoped=true and webhook.enabled=false, no webhook ingress rule is rendered (CC-0090, REQ-007)

stories.title: Keystone operator developer verifies the operator still reconciles under policy
stories.want: a Chainsaw E2E test that installs the chart with networkPolicy.enabled=true and asserts a Keystone CR reconciles to Ready (CC-0090)
stories.so_that: regressions in required egress rules (e.g. accidentally dropping DNS or tightening apiserver CIDR) surface in CI before shipping
criteria: tests/e2e/keystone-operator/network-policy-egress/chainsaw-test.yaml exists and is wired into the Chainsaw suite entry point (CC-0090, REQ-010)
The test installs the operator Helm chart with networkPolicy.enabled=true and CIDRs matching the test cluster (CC-0090, REQ-010)
The test applies a minimal Keystone CR and asserts status.conditions[Ready]=True within the existing assert timeout (CC-0090, REQ-011)
The test asserts the operator-scoped NetworkPolicy object exists in the operator's release namespace (CC-0090, REQ-010)
A catch block dumps operator pod logs and NetworkPolicy status on failure, matching the pattern in tests/e2e/keystone/network-policy/chainsaw-test.yaml (CC-0090, REQ-011)

stories.title: SRE reads documentation describing the policy contract
stories.want: a reference doc that enumerates every ingress and egress rule the NetworkPolicy creates, plus the values knobs and their defaults (CC-0090)
stories.so_that: I can audit the policy against my cluster's traffic-flow diagrams and safely tune CIDRs without reading Helm templates
criteria: docs/reference/keystone-operator-networkpolicy.md exists and lists each ingress and egress rule (direction, port(s), protocol, peer selector, source knob) (CC-0090, REQ-012)
Doc shows the default-off posture and the minimum required values snippet to turn the policy on in kind (CC-0090, REQ-012)
Doc is linked from docs/index.md and from the existing keystone-reconciler reference (CC-0090, REQ-012)
A how-to guide docs/how-to/enable-keystone-operator-networkpolicy.md walks through enabling the policy with verification steps (CC-0090, REQ-013)

requirements.description: The keystone-operator Helm chart SHALL render a NetworkPolicy targeting the operator pod when networkPolicy.enabled=true, and render no NetworkPolicy when networkPolicy.enabled=false (the default)
requirements.rationale: Opt-in NetworkPolicy prevents breaking existing installations during upgrade, while still providing the hardening path for operators who want it

scenarios.name: Enabled flag renders NetworkPolicy
scenarios.when: helm template is run with --set networkPolicy.enabled=true --set networkPolicy.kubeApiServer.cidrs[0]=10.96.0.1/32 --set networkPolicy.kubeApiServer.ports[0]=6443
scenarios.then: exactly one object of kind NetworkPolicy, apiVersion networking.k8s.io/v1, is emitted
and_then: its metadata.name is derived from the keystone-operator.fullname helper
its spec.podSelector.matchLabels equals the keystone-operator.selectorLabels template

scenarios.name: Default renders no NetworkPolicy
scenarios.when: helm template is run with no overrides
scenarios.then: zero NetworkPolicy objects are emitted
and_then: networkPolicy.enabled defaults to false in values.yaml
values.schema.json also encodes default=false

scenarios.name: Explicit disable renders no NetworkPolicy
scenarios.when: helm template is run with --set networkPolicy.enabled=false
scenarios.then: zero NetworkPolicy objects are emitted even if other networkPolicy.* values are populated
and_then: no templating errors occur when kubeApiServer values are empty and enabled=false

requirements.description: The rendered NetworkPolicy SHALL declare PolicyTypes [Ingress, Egress] so both directions default-deny and only listed rules are permitted
requirements.rationale: Declaring only Egress would still permit unrestricted ingress; explicitly listing both PolicyTypes is what turns on default-deny for each direction per the NetworkPolicy spec

scenarios.name: Both policy types set
scenarios.then: spec.policyTypes is exactly [Ingress, Egress] in that order
and_then: this matches the convention used by reconcile_networkpolicy.go for the Keystone API NetworkPolicy

scenarios.name: Selector scopes to operator pod only
scenarios.then: spec.podSelector selects only the operator pod via app.kubernetes.io/name and app.kubernetes.io/instance
and_then: no other pod in the release namespace is affected

requirements.description: The NetworkPolicy SHALL permit DNS egress on UDP/53 and TCP/53 to a configurable DNS peer (default: namespaceSelector kubernetes.io/metadata.name=kube-system + podSelector k8s-app=kube-dns)
requirements.rationale: DNS resolution is required for kubernetes.default.svc lookups and for any hostname-based client configuration. Without this rule the operator cannot resolve the kube-apiserver endpoint

scenarios.name: Default DNS rule
scenarios.when: networkPolicy.enabled=true and networkPolicy.dns is not overridden
scenarios.then: an egress rule is rendered with ports [{UDP:53}, {TCP:53}] and a to peer of namespaceSelector kubernetes.io/metadata.name=kube-system + podSelector k8s-app=kube-dns
and_then: UDP and TCP are both listed — TCP is used for responses larger than 512 bytes

scenarios.name: Custom DNS selectors
scenarios.then: the rendered DNS peer uses the overridden selectors verbatim
and_then: values.schema.json validates both selectors are object{string:string}

scenarios.name: DNS disabled via explicit false
scenarios.then: no DNS egress rule is rendered
and_then: helm template emits a comment documenting that operator may fail to resolve kubernetes.default.svc

requirements.description: The NetworkPolicy SHALL permit TCP egress to each kube-apiserver CIDR and port listed in values.networkPolicy.kubeApiServer
requirements.rationale: The operator's entire function is Kubernetes API calls; without this rule reconciliation halts and the aggregate Keystone CR Ready condition stops transitioning

scenarios.name: Single CIDR and port
scenarios.then: an egress rule is rendered with to=[{ipBlock:{cidr:10.96.0.1/32}}] and ports=[{protocol:TCP, port:6443}]
and_then: protocol is always TCP

scenarios.name: Multiple CIDRs (HA control plane)
scenarios.then: the rendered to list contains three ipBlock entries in order
and_then: no deduplication is performed — values are rendered as authored

scenarios.name: Multiple ports (managed 443 + kubelet 6443)
scenarios.then: the rendered ports list contains both entries
and_then: each port carries protocol TCP

requirements.description: values.schema.json SHALL validate networkPolicy.enabled as boolean, kubeApiServer.cidrs as array of CIDR strings, kubeApiServer.ports as array of integers in [1,65535], and all selector fields as object{string:string}
requirements.rationale: Malformed values would produce invalid NetworkPolicy manifests that kube-apiserver rejects at install time, failing obscurely. Schema validation catches these at helm install time with a clear error

scenarios.name: Invalid CIDR rejected
scenarios.when: helm install is run with kubeApiServer.cidrs=[not-a-cidr]
scenarios.then: helm rejects the install with a schema validation error mentioning the cidr pattern
and_then: no NetworkPolicy is applied to the cluster

scenarios.name: Port out of range rejected
scenarios.then: helm rejects with a schema error on the port field

scenarios.name: Wrong type rejected
scenarios.then: helm rejects with a schema error on enabled

requirements.description: The chart SHALL fail the template render when networkPolicy.enabled=true and kubeApiServer.cidrs or kubeApiServer.ports is empty — fail closed, never open
requirements.rationale: An empty egress `to` slice allows all destinations — the exact behaviour the feature is designed to prevent. The reconcile_networkpolicy.go guard for empty ingress uses the same fail-closed pattern

scenarios.name: Empty CIDRs rejected
scenarios.then: helm template fails with a {{ fail }} error naming kubeApiServer.cidrs
and_then: the error message explains that an empty list would permit all destinations

scenarios.name: Empty ports rejected
scenarios.then: helm template fails with a {{ fail }} error naming kubeApiServer.ports

scenarios.name: Disabled + empty is accepted
scenarios.then: helm template succeeds with zero NetworkPolicy objects
and_then: the fail-closed guard only triggers when enabled=true

requirements.description: When webhook.enabled=true, the NetworkPolicy SHALL render an ingress rule permitting TCP traffic on the webhook container port (9443) from the configured webhookClients peer set; when webhook.enabled=false no such rule is rendered
requirements.rationale: Default-deny ingress combined with webhook.enabled=true without this rule blocks admission webhooks, making every Keystone CR create/update fail. This is the silent-break footgun the policy must prevent

scenarios.name: Webhook rule rendered
scenarios.when: webhook.enabled=true and networkPolicy.enabled=true with default webhookClients
scenarios.then: an ingress rule on TCP 9443 is rendered with from peers derived from kubeApiServer.cidrs (the API server initiates webhook calls)
and_then: the default behaviour reuses kubeApiServer.cidrs so a single knob covers both directions

scenarios.name: Webhook disabled drops rule
scenarios.then: no ingress rule for port 9443 is rendered
and_then: this matches the namespaceScoped deployment mode which always has webhook disabled

scenarios.name: Custom webhookClients override
scenarios.then: the ingress 9443 rule uses the override verbatim

requirements.description: The NetworkPolicy SHALL render an ingress rule on TCP metrics.port ONLY when networkPolicy.allowMetricsFrom is a non-empty list of peer selectors
requirements.rationale: Metrics scraping is opt-in — not every cluster runs Prometheus, and admins may scrape via an alternate mechanism. Defaulting allowMetricsFrom to [] preserves strictest ingress posture out of the box

scenarios.name: Empty list drops metrics ingress
scenarios.then: no ingress rule for the metrics port is rendered
and_then: Prometheus cannot scrape the metrics endpoint if it runs in a namespace not otherwise matched — admins opt in explicitly

scenarios.name: Namespace-labelled peer
scenarios.then: an ingress rule on TCP metrics.port with that from peer is rendered
and_then: multiple peers may be listed; they are rendered as a single from[] list

scenarios.name: Pod-label peer
scenarios.then: the podSelector is rendered into the peer verbatim

requirements.description: The NetworkPolicy SHALL NOT render any ingress rule for port 8081 (health) — health probes are initiated by the kubelet from the host network and are exempt from NetworkPolicy enforcement in standard CNIs (Calico/Cilium)
requirements.rationale: Adding an 8081 rule would be dead code at best and attack surface at worst; documenting the exemption in a template comment and backing it with an assertion prevents future drift

scenarios.name: No health ingress rendered
scenarios.then: no ingress rule targets TCP 8081
and_then: a helm-unittest asserts the absence via notContains on ports

scenarios.name: Template comment documents the decision
scenarios.then: a comment near the ingress section explains why health probes do not need an explicit rule

requirements.description: A Chainsaw E2E test SHALL install the operator chart with networkPolicy.enabled=true and verify the operator reconciles a Keystone CR to Ready under the restricted policy
requirements.rationale: Helm-unittest proves the manifest shape; only a live-cluster test proves egress rules actually cover the traffic the operator emits. Without this, a missing port in the rendered policy would slip to production

scenarios.name: Operator reconciles under policy
scenarios.when: the test installs the chart with networkPolicy.enabled=true and the kind-specific CIDR then applies a minimal Keystone CR
scenarios.then: the CR reaches status.conditions[Ready]=True within the assert timeout
and_then: the NetworkPolicy object keystone-operator is present in the operator's release namespace
operator pod logs contain no 'i/o timeout' or 'connection refused' error lines

scenarios.name: Removing policy knob leaves behaviour unchanged
scenarios.when: the test uninstalls networkPolicy.enabled=true then re-applies with enabled=false
scenarios.then: the NetworkPolicy object is removed from the namespace by helm uninstall/upgrade

requirements.description: The E2E test SHALL dump operator pod logs and NetworkPolicy state in a catch block on assertion failure
requirements.rationale: Egress failures manifest as slow reconciliation and opaque timeouts; without log and policy dumps, debugging CI failures requires re-running the test locally, which is expensive for CC-flavoured PR reviews

scenarios.name: Logs dumped on failure
scenarios.then: the catch block runs kubectl logs on the operator pod (current and previous) and kubectl get networkpolicy -o yaml
and_then: it mirrors the catch block pattern from tests/e2e/keystone/network-policy/chainsaw-test.yaml

scenarios.name: Events included
scenarios.then: kubectl get events --sort-by=.lastTimestamp is printed (tail 30)

requirements.description: A reference doc SHALL document every ingress and egress rule the NetworkPolicy creates and every values knob that configures it
requirements.rationale: Operators need a single source of truth to audit the policy against their cluster's traffic flows; reading Helm templates is not an acceptable substitute for reference documentation

scenarios.name: Reference doc exists
scenarios.then: it tabulates each rule (direction, protocol, port, peer, configuring values key, default) and links values.schema.json fragments
and_then: the doc links to/is linked from docs/index.md under the reference section

scenarios.name: Kind defaults snippet
scenarios.when: the doc is read by a new operator
scenarios.then: a copy-pasteable values snippet enables the policy in kind
and_then: the snippet is verified by being the one used in the E2E test

requirements.description: A how-to guide SHOULD walk through enabling the policy in an existing installation with verification steps
requirements.rationale: Reference docs answer "what are the rules" but not "how do I turn them on safely"; a how-to covers the upgrade path and the post-enable verification steps

scenarios.name: How-to exists
scenarios.then: it lists prerequisites, numbered steps, verification commands, and a See Also section pointing to the reference doc
and_then: structure follows the existing docs/how-to/keystone-key-rotation.md file

scenarios.name: Troubleshooting section
scenarios.then: it lists common symptoms (reconcile timeouts, webhook TLS handshake failure) and the values knob that resolves each

tasks.title: Add networkPolicy block to values.yaml with enabled:false default, kubeApiServer.cidrs/ports empty, dns defaults (kube-system+k8s-app=kube-dns), empty allowMetricsFrom list, and empty webhookClients override block (CC-0090, REQ-001, REQ-005)

Add networkPolicy schema to values.schema.json: enabled boolean default false, kubeApiServer object with cidrs (array of CIDR-pattern strings) and ports (array of integers 1-65535), dns object with enabled/namespaceSelector/podSelector, allowMetricsFrom array of peer objects, webhookClients cidrs array; add allOf conditional requiring non-empty cidrs/ports when enabled=true (CC-0090, REQ-005, REQ-006)

Create templates/networkpolicy.yaml gated by `if .Values.networkPolicy.enabled`; render metadata with keystone-operator.fullname+labels helpers, spec.podSelector via selectorLabels, policyTypes [Ingress, Egress] (CC-0090, REQ-001, REQ-002)

Add fail-closed guards to templates/networkpolicy.yaml: `{{ fail }}` when kubeApiServer.cidrs or kubeApiServer.ports is empty — reference reconcile_networkpolicy.go:61-63 comment as convention (CC-0090, REQ-006)
Add DNS egress rule block to templates/networkpolicy.yaml: UDP+TCP 53 with configurable namespaceSelector+podSelector, gated by networkPolicy.dns.enabled (default true) (CC-0090, REQ-003)
Add kube-apiserver egress rule to templates/networkpolicy.yaml: render one egress rule with `to: []ipBlock` from cidrs and `ports: []` from ports — single rule, not one per tuple (CC-0090, REQ-004)

Add webhook ingress rule to templates/networkpolicy.yaml gated by `webhook.enabled`: TCP 9443 from peers derived from networkPolicy.webhookClients.cidrs (default: reuse kubeApiServer.cidrs) (CC-0090, REQ-007)
Add metrics ingress rule to templates/networkpolicy.yaml gated by non-empty networkPolicy.allowMetricsFrom: TCP metrics.port with from peers rendered verbatim from the list (CC-0090, REQ-008)
Add comment block in templates/networkpolicy.yaml documenting that health probe (8081) is kubelet-local and not covered by NetworkPolicy in standard CNIs; no rule added (CC-0090, REQ-009)
Author tests/networkpolicy_test.yaml helm-unittest suite with: default-off count=0, enabled-on count=1 + labels, policyTypes exact equality (CC-0090, REQ-001, REQ-002)
Extend tests/networkpolicy_test.yaml with DNS rule tests: default selectors, overridden selectors, dns.enabled=false drops rule (CC-0090, REQ-003)
Extend tests/networkpolicy_test.yaml with kube-apiserver rule tests: single CIDR+port, multiple CIDRs, multiple ports — one rule with combined lists (CC-0090, REQ-004)
Extend tests/networkpolicy_test.yaml with webhook ingress tests (enabled/disabled, custom webhookClients) and metrics ingress tests (empty list, one peer, multiple peers) (CC-0090, REQ-007, REQ-008)

Extend tests/networkpolicy_test.yaml with negative tests: enabled=true + empty cidrs triggers failedTemplate, enabled=true + empty ports triggers failedTemplate, no 8081 ingress rule ever rendered (CC-0090, REQ-006, REQ-009)
Extend tests/schema_validation_test.yaml with schema tests for networkPolicy: invalid CIDR rejected, port>65535 rejected, enabled non-boolean rejected (CC-0090, REQ-005)

Create tests/e2e/keystone-operator/network-policy-egress/ directory with 00-install-operator.yaml (Helm install with networkPolicy.enabled=true for kind CIDR 10.96.0.1/32:6443) and 01-keystone-cr.yaml (minimal Keystone CR) (CC-0090, REQ-010)

Create tests/e2e/keystone-operator/network-policy-egress/chainsaw-test.yaml: apply fixtures, assert NetworkPolicy exists in release namespace, assert Keystone CR status.conditions[Ready]=True, catch block dumping operator logs+networkpolicy+events (CC-0090, REQ-010, REQ-011)

Wire the new Chainsaw test into the operator E2E suite (tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml or the keystone-operator suite entry point — follow pattern of existing tests/e2e/keystone/network-policy registration) (CC-0090, REQ-010)

Write docs/reference/keystone-operator-networkpolicy.md: overview, default-off posture, table of all ingress and egress rules (direction/protocol/ports/peer/values-key/default), values snippet for kind, link to values.schema.json (CC-0090, REQ-012)

Write docs/how-to/enable-keystone-operator-networkpolicy.md: prerequisites (CNI supports NetworkPolicy), numbered steps (gather apiserver CIDR, update values, helm upgrade), verification (operator pod still reconciles, kubectl describe networkpolicy), troubleshooting (reconcile timeouts, webhook TLS failures), See Also pointing to reference doc (CC-0090, REQ-013)

Add reference link from docs/index.md under the reference section and a back-link from docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md (operator hardening section) to the new NetworkPolicy reference doc (CC-0090, REQ-012)

test_specifications.test_function: should render no NetworkPolicy when networkPolicy.enabled is false (default)
test_specifications.story: Cluster operator hardens keystone-operator egress via Helm
test_specifications.expected: helm-unittest with default values asserts hasDocuments { count: 0 } for templates/networkpolicy.yaml

test_specifications.test_function: should render one NetworkPolicy when networkPolicy.enabled is true
test_specifications.story: Cluster operator hardens keystone-operator egress via Helm
test_specifications.expected: helm-unittest with enabled=true and required kubeApiServer values asserts hasDocuments { count: 1 } and metadata labels match selectorLabels

test_specifications.test_function: should declare both Ingress and Egress policy types
test_specifications.story: Cluster operator hardens keystone-operator egress via Helm
test_specifications.expected: spec.policyTypes equals [Ingress, Egress] exactly

test_specifications.test_function: should render DNS egress with UDP and TCP port 53 to kube-system k8s-app=kube-dns by default
test_specifications.story: Cluster operator hardens keystone-operator egress via Helm
test_specifications.expected: spec.egress contains a rule with ports=[{UDP,53},{TCP,53}] and to=[{namespaceSelector+podSelector as defaults}]

test_specifications.test_function: should respect custom DNS selectors
test_specifications.story: Chart maintainer configures kube-apiserver endpoint for varied environments
test_specifications.expected: overriding networkPolicy.dns.namespaceSelector and podSelector renders them verbatim

test_specifications.test_function: should render a single kube-apiserver egress rule covering all configured CIDRs and ports
test_specifications.story: Chart maintainer configures kube-apiserver endpoint for varied environments
test_specifications.expected: kubeApiServer.cidrs=[10.0.0.1/32,10.0.0.2/32] and ports=[443,6443] produces one egress rule with two ipBlocks and two ports

test_specifications.test_function: should render webhook ingress rule on 9443 only when webhook.enabled=true
test_specifications.story: Cluster operator preserves webhook admission and metrics scraping
test_specifications.expected: webhook.enabled=true renders a TCP 9443 ingress rule sourced from kubeApiServer.cidrs; webhook.enabled=false renders no such rule

test_specifications.test_function: should render metrics ingress only when allowMetricsFrom is non-empty
test_specifications.story: Cluster operator preserves webhook admission and metrics scraping
test_specifications.expected: allowMetricsFrom=[] renders no TCP metrics.port rule; allowMetricsFrom=[{namespaceSelector:...}] renders one rule

test_specifications.test_function: should never render an ingress rule for TCP 8081 (health)
test_specifications.story: Cluster operator preserves webhook admission and metrics scraping
test_specifications.expected: across all value combinations, spec.ingress[].ports does not contain port=8081

test_specifications.test_function: template contains comment documenting health probe exemption
test_specifications.story: Cluster operator preserves webhook admission and metrics scraping
test_specifications.expected: rendered output contains a comment line mentioning health port 8081 is kubelet-local and exempt — asserted via matchRegex on the full template text

test_specifications.test_function: should scope podSelector to operator pod only via selectorLabels
test_specifications.story: Cluster operator hardens keystone-operator egress via Helm
test_specifications.expected: spec.podSelector.matchLabels equals {app.kubernetes.io/name: keystone-operator, app.kubernetes.io/instance: <release>} — no Keystone API labels

test_specifications.test_function: should render single egress rule combining multiple CIDRs with multiple ports
test_specifications.story: Chart maintainer configures kube-apiserver endpoint for varied environments
test_specifications.expected: cidrs=[10.0.0.1/32,10.0.0.2/32] and ports=[443,6443] renders exactly one egress rule (not a cartesian product) with two ipBlocks and two ports

test_specifications.test_function: webhook ingress rule reuses kubeApiServer.cidrs when webhookClients.cidrs is empty
test_specifications.story: Cluster operator preserves webhook admission and metrics scraping
test_specifications.expected: webhook.enabled=true, webhookClients.cidrs=[], kubeApiServer.cidrs=[10.96.0.1/32] renders ingress 9443 from ipBlock 10.96.0.1/32

test_specifications.test_function: metrics ingress accepts multiple peers
test_specifications.story: Cluster operator preserves webhook admission and metrics scraping
test_specifications.expected: allowMetricsFrom with two peer entries renders one ingress rule on TCP metrics.port with both peers in from[]

test_specifications.test_function: catch block includes NetworkPolicy YAML dump
test_specifications.story: Keystone operator developer verifies the operator still reconciles under policy
test_specifications.expected: catch block executes kubectl get networkpolicy -o yaml in the operator release namespace and prints the result

test_specifications.test_function: TestOperatorNetworkPolicyDocLinkedFromIndex
test_specifications.story: SRE reads documentation describing the policy contract
test_specifications.expected: docs/index.md contains a link to docs/reference/keystone-operator-networkpolicy.md under the reference section

test_specifications.test_function: TestOperatorNetworkPolicyHowToHasVerificationSteps
test_specifications.story: SRE reads documentation describing the policy contract
test_specifications.expected: docs/how-to/enable-keystone-operator-networkpolicy.md contains sections 'Prerequisites', 'Steps', 'Verification', 'Troubleshooting', and 'See Also' as H2 headings

test_specifications.test_function: should fail template render when enabled=true and cidrs is empty
test_specifications.story: Chart maintainer configures kube-apiserver endpoint for varied environments
test_specifications.expected: asserts asserts.failedTemplate with message containing 'kubeApiServer.cidrs must not be empty'

test_specifications.test_function: should fail template render when enabled=true and ports is empty
test_specifications.story: Chart maintainer configures kube-apiserver endpoint for varied environments
test_specifications.expected: asserts asserts.failedTemplate with message containing 'kubeApiServer.ports must not be empty'

test_specifications.test_function: should reject non-CIDR string in kubeApiServer.cidrs
test_specifications.story: Chart maintainer configures kube-apiserver endpoint for varied environments
test_specifications.expected: helm install with --set 'networkPolicy.kubeApiServer.cidrs[0]=not-a-cidr' fails schema validation citing the cidr pattern

test_specifications.test_function: should reject port above 65535
test_specifications.story: Chart maintainer configures kube-apiserver endpoint for varied environments
test_specifications.expected: helm install with --set 'networkPolicy.kubeApiServer.ports[0]=70000' fails schema validation citing maximum

test_specifications.test_function: should reject non-boolean networkPolicy.enabled
test_specifications.story: Chart maintainer configures kube-apiserver endpoint for varied environments
test_specifications.expected: helm install with --set networkPolicy.enabled=yes fails schema validation citing type boolean

test_specifications.test_function: operator reconciles Keystone CR to Ready under restricted NetworkPolicy
test_specifications.story: Keystone operator developer verifies the operator still reconciles under policy
test_specifications.expected: after helm install with networkPolicy.enabled=true and applying a minimal Keystone CR, status.conditions[Ready]=True within the assert timeout

test_specifications.test_function: NetworkPolicy object is present in operator release namespace
test_specifications.story: Keystone operator developer verifies the operator still reconciles under policy
test_specifications.expected: kubectl get networkpolicy keystone-operator in the release namespace returns a NetworkPolicy with the expected selectorLabels

test_specifications.test_function: catch block dumps logs and events on failure
test_specifications.story: Keystone operator developer verifies the operator still reconciles under policy
test_specifications.expected: asserting an impossible condition triggers catch block that prints operator pod logs (current+previous), NetworkPolicy YAML, and recent events

test_specifications.test_function: TestOperatorNetworkPolicyDocReferencesAllRules
test_specifications.story: SRE reads documentation describing the policy contract
test_specifications.expected: the doc lists DNS, kube-apiserver, webhook, and metrics rules; each values knob mentioned in values.schema.json has a row in the doc

test_specifications.test_function: TestOperatorNetworkPolicyHowToLinksReference
test_specifications.story: SRE reads documentation describing the policy contract
test_specifications.expected: docs/how-to/enable-keystone-operator-networkpolicy.md links docs/reference/keystone-operator-networkpolicy.md and is discoverable from docs/index.md

review_criteria: [blocker] helm template with default values produces zero NetworkPolicy objects (opt-in invariant)
[blocker] helm template with networkPolicy.enabled=true and empty kubeApiServer.cidrs OR empty kubeApiServer.ports fails with {{ fail }} — never renders an open egress rule
[blocker] All SHALL requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-012) have ≥1 passing helm-unittest or chainsaw assertion linked to the requirement ID
[blocker] E2E test tests/e2e/keystone-operator/network-policy-egress/chainsaw-test.yaml passes under CI: operator reaches Keystone CR Ready=True with NetworkPolicy applied
[critical] webhook.enabled=true + networkPolicy.enabled=true renders ingress 9443 — absence of this rule would silently break admission, must be covered by both helm-unittest and manual review
[critical] Generated NetworkPolicy targets ONLY the operator pod (selectorLabels), not broader labels that could match Keystone API pods and conflict with reconcile_networkpolicy.go output
[critical] values.schema.json strictly validates CIDR pattern, port range, and boolean type — invalid values fail helm install, not render
[critical] Every new task/test/doc references CC-0090 plus the requirement IDs it implements
[minor] Template follows existing chart conventions: fullname/labels/selectorLabels helpers, no inline hardcoded values
[minor] Reference doc enumerates every rule with direction/protocol/port/peer and values key — no rule undocumented
[minor] How-to doc follows docs/how-to/keystone-key-rotation.md structure (prerequisites → steps → verification → see also)
[minor] Template has a comment explaining why no 8081 (health) ingress rule is needed, to prevent future drift
implementation_notes:
Architecture: the keystone-operator pod is deployed by the Helm chart, not reconciled by the operator itself. Consequently the NetworkPolicy for the operator is a STATIC Helm template — NOT a new sub-reconciler, despite the issue description mentioning reconcile_networkpolicy.go. The existing reconcile_networkpolicy.go continues to manage the NetworkPolicy for Keystone API pods (CC-0039) and is not modified by this feature.

Correct chart path: operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/ (the issue description's 'operators/keystone/chart/templates/' is stale).

Default-off posture: networkPolicy.enabled defaults to false to avoid breaking upgrades in clusters whose kube-apiserver CIDR or DNS layout does not match our defaults. Existing reference behaviour (ServiceMonitor-style opt-in) is the template.

Fail-closed guards: mirroring reconcile_networkpolicy.go:61-63, empty cidrs/ports trigger `{{ fail }}` rather than rendering an open egress rule. An egress `to: []` permits all destinations, which would defeat the entire feature.

Egress set: DNS (UDP+TCP 53 to kube-dns) + kube-apiserver (TCP to configured CIDRs and ports). The operator does not talk directly to Keystone pods, MariaDB, Memcached, webhooks, or metrics backends — it only issues Kubernetes API calls. Events, leases, logs-via-kubectl, pod/cm/secret CRUD all flow through kube-apiserver.

Ingress set: webhook 9443 (from kube-apiserver CIDRs, gated by webhook.enabled) and optionally metrics.port (from explicit allowMetricsFrom peers). Health probe 8081 is NOT exposed because kubelet health probes originate from the host network and bypass NetworkPolicy in standard CNIs (Calico, Cilium).

ASCII Architecture Diagram:

  +---------------------------+            +---------------------------+
  |  kube-apiserver           |<-- TCP --->|  keystone-operator pod    |
  |  (operator makes API      |  443/6443  |  (selectorLabels match)   |
  |  calls, receives webhook) |            |                           |
  +---------------------------+            |  - manager container      |
        |                                   |  - metrics :8080          |
        |  TCP 9443 webhook                 |  - webhook :9443          |
        +---------------------------------->|  - health  :8081 (host)   |
                                            +---------------------------+
                                                     |   ^
                                                     |   | optional TCP metrics.port
                                                     v   | from allowMetricsFrom
                                             +---------------------------+
                                             |  kube-dns (UDP+TCP 53)    |
                                             +---------------------------+

Data Flow Diagram:

  Helm install
    -> values.yaml + values.schema.json validated
    -> if networkPolicy.enabled: fail-closed guard on empty cidrs/ports
    -> render NetworkPolicy { podSelector: operator, egress: [dns, apiserver], ingress: [webhook?, metrics?] }
    -> kube-apiserver admits manifest
    -> CNI enforces policy on operator pod egress/ingress

Reuse pattern from reconcile_networkpolicy.go:
  - PolicyTypes declaration order [Ingress, Egress]
  - Port rendering via protocol+port tuples
  - namespaceSelector+podSelector peer composition
  - fail-closed on empty rule inputs

Deferred items (out of scope, documented here to prevent scope creep during implementation):
  - Moving the operator NetworkPolicy to a sub-reconciler. Rationale: the operator pod is installed by Helm, not owned by a Keystone CR. Revisit if a second cluster-scoped operator-pod-owned resource appears.
  - FQDN/DNS-name-based egress rules (e.g. to flux-managed Git endpoints). Rationale: the operator does not currently call out beyond kube-apiserver. Revisit only when a new call pattern is added.
  - Pre-configured apiserver detection via helm lookup. Rationale: helm lookup does not work in helm template and is fragile across deploy tools (Flux/ArgoCD). Users pass cidrs explicitly — the how-to doc provides the `kubectl get endpoints kubernetes` snippet.
  - CiliumNetworkPolicy / Calico GlobalNetworkPolicy CRs. Rationale: standard v1 NetworkPolicy covers the acceptance criteria and is portable; CNI-specific CRs are a future tightening.
